Description: Legacy systems often contain hotspots that concentrate change and defects.
Legacy systems frequently accumulate clusters of technical debt.
Use the Strangler Fig pattern to gradually replace legacy components.
Place an anti-corruption layer between new code and legacy subsystems.
Changes in hotspots are more likely to introduce regressions.
Technical debt clusters make safe changes harder and more error-prone.
Incremental replacement with clear seams reduces regression risk.
Isolating legacy behavior behind adapters reduces the blast radius of changes.
